## Bicycles and tricycles

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Streamliner&action=edit&section=30 "Edit section: Bicycles and tricycles")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/06/Bicolor_Velomobile.jpg/250px-Bicolor_Velomobile.jpg)](/wiki/File:Bicolor_Velomobile.jpg)

A partially enclosed three-wheeled velomobile (2018)

[Bicycle fairings](/wiki/Bicycle_fairing "Bicycle fairing") help to streamline the vehicle and the rider. [Human powered](/wiki/Human-powered_transport#Human-powered_vehicles_(HPVs) "Human-powered transport") [upright](/wiki/Safety_bicycle "Safety bicycle") and [recumbent bicycles](/wiki/Recumbent_bicycle "Recumbent bicycle") and [tricycles](/wiki/Tricycle#Recumbent "Tricycle") termed [velomobiles](/wiki/Velomobile "Velomobile") that are partially or completely enclosed for [aerodynamic](/wiki/Aerodynamics "Aerodynamics") advantage and weather protection take streamlining even further. Although many velomobiles are recreational, two-wheeled velomobiles have set a number of [cycling speed records](/wiki/List_of_cycling_records "List of cycling records").[[63]](#cite_note-63)

## Ships

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Streamliner&action=edit&section=31 "Edit section: Ships")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/09/Kalakala.jpg/250px-Kalakala.jpg)](/wiki/File:Kalakala.jpg)

The *MV Kalakala* ferry in 1962

The [Streamline Moderne](/wiki/Streamline_Moderne "Streamline Moderne")–style automobile/passenger ferry *[MV Kalakala](/wiki/MV_Kalakala "MV Kalakala")* received its streamlining during a 1933–1935 reconstruction. The ship operated in [Puget Sound](/wiki/Puget_Sound "Puget Sound") near the northwestern coast of the U.S. state of [Washington](/wiki/Washington_(state) "Washington (state)") until 1967. It was scrapped in 2015.[[64]](#cite_note-64)

## Sterling Streamliner diners

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Streamliner&action=edit&section=32 "Edit section: Sterling Streamliner diners")]

Many American roadside [diners](/wiki/Diners "Diners") built since the 1930s have had streamlined exteriors and interiors.[[65]](#cite_note-65) In 1939, Roland Stickney designed a diner named the *Sterling Streamliner*. Built by the [John B. Judkins Company](/wiki/John_B._Judkins_Company "John B. Judkins Company"), a firm that also made custom car bodies,[[66]](#cite_note-Judkins-66) the [prefabricated](/wiki/Prefabrication "Prefabrication") diner's production ceased in 1942 at the beginning of American involvement in World War II.

The rounded shapes of one or both ends of the *Sterling Streamliner* diners resembled the sloping curved nose of the *Burlington Zephyr'*s streamlined silver locomotive.[[66]](#cite_note-Judkins-66)[[67]](#cite_note-67)
One such *Sterling Streamliner* with two rounded ends was built in 1940 and installed as the *Jimmy Evans Flyer* in [New Bedford, Massachusetts](/wiki/New_Bedford,_Massachusetts "New Bedford, Massachusetts"). In the 1960s, the building was moved to the village of [Pocasset](/wiki/Pocasset,_Massachusetts "Pocasset, Massachusetts") in the town of [Bourne, Massachusetts](/wiki/Bourne,_Massachusetts "Bourne, Massachusetts"), on [Cape Cod](/wiki/Cape_Cod "Cape Cod"), where it was named the *My Tin Diner*.[[68]](#cite_note-Flyer-68)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1e/Modern_diner.jpg/250px-Modern_diner.jpg)](/wiki/File:Modern_diner.jpg)

The Modern Diner in May 2010

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d9/Salemdiner.jpg/250px-Salemdiner.jpg)](/wiki/File:Salemdiner.jpg)

Salem Diner, in Massachusetts

In 2000, an [arsonist](/wiki/Arson "Arson") severely damaged the *My Tin Diner* when he set it on fire. In 2003, the structure was moved into a field next to the *Handy Hill Creamery* near Hix Bridge Road in [Westport, Massachusetts](/wiki/Westport,_Massachusetts "Westport, Massachusetts"), while plans were being made to restore it to working condition. However, although restoration began, it was not completed. Visible from the Road, the derelict structure was the only *Sterling Streamliner* with two rounded ends known to still survive in 2019.[[68]](#cite_note-Flyer-68)

Only one *Sterling Streamliner* was open for business in 2020: the [Modern Diner](/wiki/Modern_Diner "Modern Diner") in [Pawtucket, Rhode Island](/wiki/Pawtucket,_Rhode_Island "Pawtucket, Rhode Island"). That structure, which was placed on the [National Register of Historic Places](/wiki/National_Register_of_Historic_Places "National Register of Historic Places") in 1978, has both a rounded sloped end and a flat vertical end. Although the building's roof was once silver, it is now [maroon](/wiki/Maroon "Maroon").[[69]](#cite_note-69)
